tip.label: Helpful hints
tip.translation:
Barcha modal fe'llar:
• faqat bitta shaklga ega — I/you/he/she/it/we/they **may** write an e-mail.
• asosiy fe'lning boshlang'ich shakli (bare infinitive) bilan keladi — You should **call** Stella.
• infinitive shaklida bo'lmaydi.

Yarim modallar (Semi-modals) — modallarga o'xshab ishlatiladigan iboralar:
• ought to (ought not to)
• have to (don't have to)
• need to (don't need to / needn't)

Modallarga o'xshab, _ought to_ o'zgarmaydi. _Have to_ va _need to_ esa odatdagi fe'llar kabi shaxs va zamonga qarab o'zgaradi va infinitivga ega.
tip.translation_ru:
Все модальные глаголы:
• имеют только одну форму — I/you/he/she/it/we/they **may** write an e-mail.
• используются с инфинитивом без частицы to (bare infinitive) — You should **call** Stella.
• не имеют инфинитива.

Полумодальные глаголы (Semi-modals) — фразы, используемые как модальные:
• ought to (ought not to)
• have to (don't have to)
• need to (don't need to / needn't)

Как и модальные, _ought to_ не изменяется. _Have to_ и _need to_ изменяются по лицу и времени, как обычные глаголы, и имеют инфинитив.

tip.label: Watch out!
tip.translation:
Modal fe'llar bilan so'roq gaplarni shunday tuzamiz:
✓ **Can you** understand what he's saying?

Modal fe'llarni passiv gap tuzishda shunday ishlatamiz:
✓ The address **should be written** clearly on the front of the envelope.
tip.translation_ru:
Вопросительные предложения с модальными глаголами мы строим так:
✓ **Can you** understand what he's saying?

Модальные глаголы в пассивном залоге используются так:
✓ The address **should be written** clearly on the front of the envelope.

cells: _Tom **could** read when he was two years old._
cells_uz: _Tom **could** read when he was two years old._
cells_ru: _Tom **could** read when he was two years old._

tip.label: Watch out!
tip.translation:
Boshqa zamонlarda _be able to_ shaklini ishlatamiz:
✓ It's useful **to be able to** order things by e-mail. (infinitive)
✓ Soon, I'**ll be able to** speak Italian quite well. (future)
✓ **Have** you **been able to** speak English for a long time? (present perfect)
tip.translation_ru:
Для других времён мы используем форму _be able to_:
✓ It's useful **to be able to** order things by e-mail. (инфинитив)
✓ Soon, I'**ll be able to** speak Italian quite well. (будущее время)
✓ **Have** you **been able to** speak English for a long time? (present perfect)

cells: _**Can / Could / May** I use the phone?_
cells_uz: _**Can / Could / May** I use the phone?_
cells_ru: _**Can / Could / May** I use the phone?_

cells: _You **can / may** send the fax when you like._
cells_uz: _You **can / may** send the fax when you like._
cells_ru: _You **can / may** send the fax when you like._

tip.label: Watch out!
tip.translation: _May_ so'rashda _could_'dan ko'ra ko'proq, _could_ esa _can_'dan ko'ra ko'proq rasmiy (muloyim) hisoblanadi.
tip.translation_ru: _May_ является более вежливым, чем _could_, а _could_ — более вежливым, чем _can_, при просьбе о разрешении.
